title: Creative Detail and Decision Review
course: Agentic Development with GitHub Copilot CLI, LakeBank, SyteLine, and MCP
prerequisites: Learner can start a safe GitHub Copilot CLI session.
Learner has a planning artifact ready for review.
summary: Before implementation, convert one plan into at least two viable options and review them critically.
whyItMatters: A single unchallenged plan often hides risk.
Generating at least two options improves design quality and exposes assumptions early.
Rubber Duck and persona review help teams choose safer implementation paths before coding starts.
howItFits: Module 8 expands that plan into multiple options, critiques those options, and records a decision.
Next you will use this reviewed decision in Module 9 test-first implementation.
learningObjectives: Generate at least two implementation options before selecting a direction.
Use Rubber Duck as a skeptical reviewer mode.
Run persona-based reviews with repo-specific examples.
Compare options by risk, testability, and delivery impact.
Document the selected option with rationale and risk note.
Keep all review output read-only and planning-only.
Decide whether implementation can start or should remain blocked.
keyConceptsMarkdown: Do not decide from a single idea.
Produce Option A and Option B before choosing.
### Concept 2: Rubber Duck is skeptical reviewer mode
Rubber Duck review challenges hidden assumptions, weak evidence, and missing safeguards.
It is a review mode, not a coding mode.
### Concept 3: Persona review should be repo-specific
Use persona reviewers that match your repo context, such as:
- support for diagnostics and incident handling
- QA for test and edge-case coverage
Require concrete repo-specific examples in feedback.
### Concept 4: Decision output must be explicit
mechanicsMarkdown: Use one prompt to create option comparisons and review output in a single artifact.
Use follow-up prompts to tighten findings, not to implement code.
variationNotesMarkdown: In product repos, prioritize architecture consistency and regression safety.
In customer-modification repos, prioritize validation depth and customer-impact containment.
instructorDemoMarkdown: ## Demo step 1: turn one plan into two options
Create Option A and Option B for the same task.
## Demo step 2: run Rubber Duck and persona review
Use skeptical and role-based critique to compare both options.
Choose one option and capture tradeoffs, risk note, and unresolved questions.

lab.title: Produce and review two implementation options
beforeYouStart: I opened a terminal in the target repo.
I have a current planning artifact.
I know this lab is read-only and no implementation is allowed.

prompts.label: Lab prompt
promptText: We are practicing creative detail and decision review for an internal course.
- Do not change repo instructions, plugins, or MCP configuration.
Reference the source plan and summary scope.
Describe approach, expected benefits, key risks, and test strategy.
Describe approach, expected benefits, key risks, and test strategy.
Challenge assumptions, missing evidence, missing tests, and hidden risk.
Include at least three personas and give repo-specific examples.
Compare Option A and B on complexity, risk, testability, support impact, and rollback clarity.
State which option is selected and why.
State the top risk of the selected option and mitigation plan.
List questions that require human review before implementation.

prompts.label: Optional decision-tightening prompt
promptText: Strengthen the selected-option rationale and remove weak or repetitive findings.

verificationChecklist: Did Copilot produce at least two options?
Did Rubber Duck feedback challenge assumptions and risk?
Did persona feedback include repo-specific examples?
Did the artifact compare options with clear criteria?
Did the selected option include rationale and risk note?

evidence.summaryPrompt: Write 3-5 sentences describing the chosen option and why it is safer or more effective.
reflectionQuestions: What did Option B reveal that Option A missed?
Which reviewer feedback changed the final decision?
What must be confirmed before implementation starts?

skillCheck.question: Why require at least two options before deciding?
skillCheck.expectedAnswer: It reduces tunnel vision and exposes tradeoffs that a single-option plan can hide.

skillCheck.question: What is Rubber Duck mode in this module?
skillCheck.expectedAnswer: A skeptical reviewer mode that challenges assumptions and risk before implementation.

skillCheck.question: Why should persona feedback be repo-specific?
skillCheck.expectedAnswer: Repo-specific examples make feedback actionable and reduce generic noise.

skillCheck.question: What should a comparison matrix include?
skillCheck.expectedAnswer: Complexity, risk, testability, support impact, and rollback clarity.

skillCheck.question: What must accompany the selected option?
skillCheck.expectedAnswer: Clear rationale, rejected-alternative reasoning, and a risk note.

skillCheck.question: Can this module include implementation changes?
skillCheck.expectedAnswer: No. It is planning and review only.

skillCheck.question: When should implementation remain blocked?
skillCheck.expectedAnswer: When major risks, missing evidence, or unresolved human decisions remain.

summaryMarkdown: In this module, you transformed one plan into multiple options and selected a direction using structured review.
You used Rubber Duck and persona critique to improve quality before implementation.
readinessChecklist: I can produce and compare at least two options.
I can run Rubber Duck skeptical review.
I can require repo-specific persona examples.
I can document selected option rationale and risk note.
I can identify when implementation should stay blocked.
commonMistakes: Choosing the first option without comparison.
Using generic persona feedback with no repo detail.
Treating review output as implementation approval.
review.nextModuleTeaser:
Module 9: Test-Driven Agentic Development

Next you will define tests first so implementation stays constrained and verifiable.
